title: Secure Ingestion
objective: Accept TXT, ZIP, and metadata CSV inputs through a bounded, content-validated, fail-closed intake boundary without workspace escape, uncontrolled resource consumption, or payload leakage.
scope: Declared-role TXT, ZIP, and metadata CSV intake with extension, supplied MIME, and content agreement
Strict UTF-8 decoding and Unicode NFC validation
Pre-extraction ZIP member audit and bounded streaming extraction
Versioned size, member, ratio, path, token, and line limits
Server-generated asset identifiers and controlled display labels
CSV formula, HTML, newline, path, and log injection defenses
Content-free error codes and rejected-input cleanup
Deterministic malicious fixtures, property tests, fuzz checks, and clean-clone evidence
non_goals: Metadata semantics, corpus inventory, or rights decisions
Job queue, retention scheduler, or production deployment
R stylo execution or any scientific result
Pinocchio or other research corpus ingestion
PDF, DOCX, EPUB, TEI, OCR, nested archives, or general collation
Runtime AI, login, analytics, permanent project storage, or lemmata.app integration

acceptance.criterion: Each upload has an explicit corpus-text, corpus-archive, or metadata-table role; extension and any client-supplied MIME must agree with that role, MIME absence never substitutes for content validation, and the role parser rejects invalid UTF-8, non-NFC, known binary/document signatures, ZIP masquerades, malformed, empty, or over-limit payloads.

Zip-slip, absolute paths, drive paths, symlinks, hardlinks, nested archives, duplicate normalized names, reserved names, and archive-bomb fixtures are rejected before extraction and cannot escape the fresh destination.
Compressed size, expanded size, member count, compression ratio, path length, line count, token count, and token length are enforced from one versioned limit profile without silent downgrade.
Storage names and asset identifiers are server-generated; user filenames remain controlled display labels, and CSV formula, HTML, newline, path, and log injection fixtures fail closed.

Every rejection returns a content-free stable error code, creates no analysis state, and leaves no payload copy in Delta-managed temporary directories, application session state, logs, or exception output; infrastructure-level retention remains P005/P014 evidence.
Deterministic malicious fixtures, bounded property/fuzz tests, disk canary scans, full verification, and a clean-clone rerun all pass before P003 is closed.
